title: OpenAI ships GPT-5 with native tool use and 1M context
description: OpenAI released GPT-5 with native tool use, 1M token context window, and significant improvements in reasoning and code generation. Available via API immediately. Pricing: $15/1M input tokens, $60/1M output.
actionNote: Worth benchmarking against Claude for LawTrades AI features. May offer cost advantages for high-volume contract parsing.

title: Vercel ships AI SDK 4.0 with streaming agents and persistent memory
description: Vercel's AI SDK 4.0 adds first-class support for streaming agent responses, persistent agent memory across sessions, and built-in tool orchestration. Works with Next.js 15 out of the box.
actionNote: Directly relevant to LawTrades AI features and The Marketplace frontend stack. Check compatibility with current Next.js version.

title: Anthropic releases Claude 4 Sonnet with 200K context and computer use v2
description: Anthropic shipped Claude 4 Sonnet, improving code generation by ~40% on SWE-bench and adding computer use v2 with better UI interaction accuracy. Context stays at 200K. Available on API now — same claude-sonnet-4 slug.
actionNote: This is Día's current model. Performance improvements in code gen directly benefit sub-agent coding tasks.

title: Linear ships Cycles v2 — AI sprint planning with automatic priority scoring
description: Linear released Cycles v2 with AI-powered sprint planning. The AI scores issue priority based on dependencies, blockers, team velocity, and due dates — then auto-populates cycles. Also adds a new 'Focus Mode' that hides all non-cycle issues.
actionNote: Ilolo uses Linear daily. Cycles v2 could meaningfully reduce sprint planning overhead. Worth demoing to the LawTrades team.

title: BigLaw firms rolling out autonomous AI agents for contract review — marketplace play for LawTrades
description: Several BigLaw firms (Kirkland, Latham, Skadden) announced autonomous AI agents for contract review and due diligence, trained on their own deal history. The agents flag issues, suggest redlines, and estimate negotiation outcomes. Still requires senior attorney sign-off.
actionNote: Competitive intel — law firms are getting agentic AI. LawTrades should be the marketplace that connects this talent/tech to companies.

title: Cursor 3: Agent-first coding with parallel agents across cloud, SSH, and local
description: Cursor shipped v3.0 on April 2, rebuilding its interface from scratch around AI agents. The new Agents Window lets devs run dozens of agents in parallel across local repos, cloud VMs, worktrees, and remote SSH. It also adds structured MCP tool calls and an agent task queue. A fundamental shift: from IDE that assists to workspace that executes.
actionNote: Ilolo uses Cursor daily — v3.0 is a big deal. Worth upgrading and exploring the Agents Window for LawTrades frontend work.

title: Microsoft open-sources Agent Governance Toolkit — covers all 10 OWASP Agentic risks
description: Microsoft released the Agent Governance Toolkit, a 7-package open-source system for governing autonomous AI agents: policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and reliability engineering. Covers all 10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 risks. Designed for agents operating without human-in-the-loop supervision.
actionNote: Directly relevant to Ilolo's security focus and LawTrades AI. Worth reviewing for any agentic features in the marketplace — especially if processing legal docs autonomously.

title: Polymarket arbitrage bot made $152K in 6 months on $30-50K capital — full anatomy
description: Deep analysis of a Polymarket bot (0x7a3f...) that executed ~50,200 trades over 6 months, capturing an average 1.2% spread on Yes/No mispricings. Net profit ~$152K after gas. 94.2% win rate. Capital turnover 250-400x. Gas cost on Polygon only $2,100 total. Key stack: Python, py-clob-client, Polygon WebSocket, CLOB API. Competitive spreads now 0.3-0.8% on major markets — long-tail and new markets still offer 1-2%+.
actionNote: Core research for Ilolo's $100 Polymarket bot side project. Temporal arbitrage on 15-min BTC/ETH markets is best starting strategy at low capital. Full research in workspace/research/polymarket-research.md

title: Columbia Law study: 'Informed' Polymarket traders netted $143M since 2024
description: Researchers at Columbia Law School and University of Haifa analyzed 210,000+ Polymarket trades (2024-2026) and found 'informed' (possibly insider) traders netted $143M. Example: ricosuave666 made $155K betting on Israeli strikes on Iran hours before they happened. Polymarket just banned trades using stolen confidential information but enforcement is unclear. Regulatory pressure incoming from CFTC and state AGs.
actionNote: Important regulatory context for Polymarket bot project. Stick to pure arb/market-making (risk-neutral) for lowest regulatory exposure. Avoid large directional bets.
